title: Matching lower bound establishing worst-case tightness

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The problem asks for the **worst-case** complexity, so a $\Theta$ (or at least
$\Omega$) result is required, not only an upper bound. A worst-case instance family must be
exhibited and analyzed.

**What must be shown.** A sequence of consistent polycyclic presentations and input words on
which the collection algorithm provably incurs $\Omega(T(\ell,n,b))$ bit operations, matching
the upper bound from the other sub-lemmas.

**Concrete starting point.** $G=\langle g_1,g_2\mid g_2^{g_1}=g_2^{2}\rangle$ with input
$g_1^{-\ell}\,g_2\,g_1^{\ell}$ forces output exponent $2^\ell$ (bit-length $\Theta(\ell)$), so
merely *writing* the normal form costs $\Omega(\ell)$ bits and computing it costs at least the
arithmetic on $\Theta(\ell)$-bit integers. Generalize: (a) scale to Hirsch length $n$ via a
chain of conjugations to expose the $n$-dependence; (b) increase relation exponents to expose
the $b$-dependence. 

**Subtlety.** The lower bound must be against the collection algorithm *as defined* (collection
from the left moving lower indices left). If a stronger claim is made (no collection strategy
can do better), it must be argued separately. State precisely which claim is proved.
